Heat Wave Grips Odisha: Bhubaneswar Records 38.4°C at 11:30 AM Amid High Humidity
Western Odisha bore the brunt of the heat. Jharsuguda crossed 41°C by 11:30 AM, recording 41.8°C. Sambalpur reported 41.2°C and Hirakud 40.6°C.
Bhubaneswar: Coastal Odisha is reeling under an intense heat wave as high temperatures combined with high humidity made conditions unbearable on Thursday.
At 11:30 AM, Bhubaneswar recorded a temperature of 38.4°C. With humidity at 56%, the heat index or “feels like” temperature soared to around 53°C.
For comparison, the temperature at the same time last night was 35.4°C.
Western Odisha bore the brunt of the heat. Jharsuguda crossed 41°C by 11:30 AM, recording 41.8°C. Sambalpur reported 41.2°C and Hirakud 40.6°C. Keonjhar recorded 39.4°C.
The India Meteorological Department has warned that the severe heat will persist across the state for the next five days, with no respite expected from the coast to the north, west, and south.

title: Jharsuguda Sizzling With 41.8°C At 11:30 AM As Severe Heatwave Sweeps Odisha - Ommcom News
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
full_text:
Bhubaneswar: Western Odisha is reeling under severe heatwave conditions as Jharsuguda recorded the highest temperature of 41.8°C at 11:30 am today, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
Temperatures remained extremely high in other parts of the state as well, with Sambalpur recording 41.2°C, Hirakud 40.6°C, Keonjhar 39.4°C, Bhubaneswar 38.4°C, and Rourkela 38.0°C.
In Bhubaneswar, the temperature touched 38.4°C at 11:30 am, a sharp rise from 35.4°C recorded at the same time yesterday. With relative humidity at 56%, the heat index (feels-like temperature) reached approximately 53°C, making the conditions extremely oppressive.
People have been advised to stay hydrated, avoid direct sun exposure during peak hours, and take extra precautions, especially vulnerable groups such as children, the elderly, and outdoor workers.
